name: api-designer
description: OpenAPI-first API Designer for visual editing, governance validation, and AI readiness analysis in VS Code.

languageModelTools.name: api-designer_validateApiSpec
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Validates an OpenAPI specification. Supports standard OpenAPI validation and governance validation by reportId (ai-readiness, owasp, rest-api-readiness).

apiSpec.description: The OpenAPI specification to validate (can be YAML/JSON string or object).

fileUri.description: Optional: URI of the file containing the API specification. If provided, this takes precedence over apiSpec.

reportId.description: Optional: Governance report flow to run. If provided with fileUri, returns unified violations for that report. For ai-readiness, includes LLM findings when available.

languageModelTools.name: api-designer_openInApiDesigner
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Opens the API Designer panel for an OpenAPI file or the active editor.

fileUri.description: Optional: URI of the OpenAPI file to open in API Designer.

viewType.description: Optional: View type to open (for example: create).

languageModelTools.name: api-designer_resolveAIFinding
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Removes a resolved AI-readiness finding from cached AI analysis report data for the active API file and updates the stored hash.

rule.description: The finding rule identifier (for example: Rule 5.1).

filePath.description: Optional absolute filesystem path of the API file.

fileUri.description: Optional file URI of the API file. Used when filePath is not provided.

pathSegments.description: Optional path segments identifying where the finding occurred.

message.description: Optional finding message to disambiguate similar findings.

apiDesigner.debugLog.description: Enable printing debug messages to the API Designer output channel

apiDesigner.notifyOnOpen.description: Show a notification when opening an OpenAPI file, offering to open it in API Designer.

apiDesigner.ai.provider.description: AI provider to use for code generation and assistance

apiDesigner.spectral.rulesetFolders.description: GitHub folder URLs or local paths containing Spectral rulesets. Adding a new folder here triggers the extension to list the contained rulesets for selection.
apiDesigner.spectral.rulesetFolders.markdownDescription:
GitHub folder URLs or local paths containing Spectral rulesets. When you add a new entry, the extension automatically scans the folder and lets you choose which rulesets to enable.

If this setting is empty, the extension uses bundled rulesets from `<extension>/spectral-rulesets` by default.

apiDesigner.spectral.selectedRulesets.description: Rulesets discovered from configured folders and whether they are enabled for selection and analysis.

sourceFolder.description: The source folder URL or path the ruleset was discovered from

rulesetContentPath.description: Optional JSONPath inside the YAML where the ruleset content resides

enabled.description: Whether the ruleset is enabled for governance/ruleset selection workflows

scripts.build: pnpm clean && pnpm run copyFonts && webpack --mode production --devtool hidden-source-map && pnpm run package && pnpm run postbuild
scripts.pretest: pnpm run compile-tests && pnpm run compile && pnpm run lint
scripts.e2e-test-setup: npx extest get-vscode -c 1.81.1 && npx extest get-chromedriver -c 1.81.1 && npx extest install-vsix -f $(ls vsix/*.vsix)
scripts.e2e-test: pnpm run compile-tests && pnpm exec playwright test
